summary: Three reviewers (architecture, quality, systems) submitted; the reality review file is absent on disk — dispatcher-provided hints (M1: wrong test class name TestSources vs TestListSources; M2: stale inferred-quarantine language at 3-4 sites) are folded into the warnings list but should be re-run if reality review delivery is recovered. Plan 16 (index) is a clean three-slice map with no hidden scope; all three reviewers say GO. Plan 16a (backend) is technically sound — additive-only ClassVars, layer-clean, TDD-shaped, rev-2 quarantine fix correct, Tier-1 crash discipline preserved on data_trust_tier — but two convergent themes block a clean GO. (1) UNDERSCOPING / NO-LEGACY violation around trust.py: architecture B1 (MAJOR) frames it as a No-Legacy breach because trust.py's docstring commits to same-commit deletion when the ClassVar lands; systems W4 frames the same gap as a missing finish line for the 'catalog is reference, not toolkit' redesign (1 of 58 plugins annotated, no named successor phase). Memory feedback_repeated_out_of_scope_is_underscoping says third-tasking 'no X' is a flag, not authority. This is the load-bearing concern. (2) CLOSED-ENUM / VOCABULARY DRIFT without mechanical guards: architecture W5 catches _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG.get(determinism) silently dropping future enum values; systems M1 catches missing vocabulary-membership test on declared audit_characteristics. Different surfaces, same archetype — silent pass-through that destroys the audit-readability surface for the same Linda persona the redesign serves. (3) Convergent textual fix: stale 'inferred-from-quarantine' language at 3-4 sites (architecture W1/W2/W3, quality NIT-1, dispatcher-Reality M2) is documentation-only and trivial. No security or layer-import findings. No defensive-programming violations except the closed-enum .get() flagged in W5. Phase 16a can ship with these changes; the operator must decide between Option A (author all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins + delete trust.py in 16a) and Option B (sibling-phase 16a-supplement; rewrite trust.py docstring to defer the trigger to authoring completion) before implementation starts.
reviewers_missing_note: 16-phase-7-catalog-reshape.reality-review.json was not on disk at synthesis time. Dispatcher provided two reality-finding hints (M1 wrong test class name; M2 stale inferred-quarantine language) which are folded into M2/W1 below. If the reality review is recovered, re-synthesize — there may be codebase-verified findings the other three reviewers cannot catch (e.g. fixture-shape errors like the Phase 2 review's NodeSpec/OutputSpec TypeErrors).

convergence_findings.title: trust.py deletion commitment fires but deletion is not in scope — two sources of truth + no named finish line
convergence_findings.evidence: trust.py:31-32 contains the verbatim commitment: 'When Phase 7 adds data_trust_tier: ClassVar to plugin base classes, delete this module entirely and replace classify_plugin() callers' (verified on disk 2026-05-17). audit_readiness/service.py:171 still carries the # TODO(phase-7) comment; service.py:172 still calls classify_plugin(kind, name). 16a-phase-7a-backend.md scope-boundary explicitly defers 'Per-plugin prose authoring for every plugin in the codebase. That is an incremental documentation task; this plan ships the schema + one canonical example and intentionally stops there.' 00-implementation-roadmap.md line 157: '7A → 7B → 7C   (independent track; can start any time after 1B)' — no 7D, no documentation sprint, no successor phase. Phase 7A authors 1 of 58 plugins (the canonical csv_source.py); the remaining EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS (llm, web_scrape, rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield) and EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_SINKS (azure_blob, chroma_sink, database, dataverse) ship with data_trust_tier=None and trust.py allowlist as the only source of truth.
convergence_findings.convergence_signal: Architecture frames this as a No-Legacy policy violation (CLAUDE.md: 'No version checks, feature flags for old behaviour, adapter/wrapper/proxy shims — Change all call sites in the same commit.'). Systems frames it as Tragedy-of-the-Commons + Deferred-Completion archetype, citing memory feedback_repeated_out_of_scope_is_underscoping. The two perspectives are the same underlying defect from different lenses: a load-bearing commitment in code is being technically-satisfied by adding the ClassVar with None defaults, while the substantive obligation (every EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugin carrying a real value, trust.py deleted, audit_readiness panel reading data_trust_tier directly) is deferred to no named phase.
convergence_findings.resolution: See operator-decision list below. Option A (preferred per No-Legacy): author data_trust_tier on all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins in Task 5 (alongside csv_source, not instead of it), delete trust.py and replace classify_plugin() callers in the same commit. Adds ~3-4 commits to Phase 7A but discharges the load-bearing commitment. Option B (acceptable with operator override): rewrite trust.py:31-35 docstring to say 'when all EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins have data_trust_tier authored' (making the trigger condition truthful), file a filigree blocker 16a-supplement tracking the bulk authoring, and acknowledge that this is a deliberate deferred-completion. Option C (not recommended): delete the deletion-commitment language outright, keep trust.py permanently — contradicts the design intent and CLAUDE.md.

convergence_findings.title: Closed vocabulary, free-text storage, no mechanical guard — applies to both Determinism→flag mapping AND author-declared audit_characteristics
convergence_findings.evidence: Architecture W5: _derive_audit_characteristics uses _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG.get(determinism) with implicit None fallback. Determinism is a 6-value closed StrEnum in contracts/enums.py. If a 7th value is added without updating the map, .get() silently returns None — the inference is dropped, no crash, no test failure. CLAUDE.md Defensive Programming: Forbidden lists this exact pattern as the 'silent pass-through that destroys the audit trail.' 00-implementation-roadmap.md §H5 names this as a known governance pattern. Systems M1: capability_tags: frozenset[str] and audit_characteristics: frozenset[str] are free-text authoring surfaces with no enum/Literal constraint. A typo ('io-read' for 'io_read', 'quarentine' for 'quarantine') silently produces a missing icon in the catalog UI. The frontend's auditCharacteristics.ts (16b scope) maps known strings to icons; unknown strings no-op. No CI gate asserts the declared strings are members of a known vocabulary.
convergence_findings.convergence_signal: Same archetype, two surfaces. Architecture caught the enum-side (closed enum, dict .get() drops growth); systems caught the string-side (free-text declared values, no membership check). Both are silent failures on the same audit-characteristic composition path. Both fixes are small (~10-30 lines): subscript access + exhaustivity test for the enum side; VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant + iteration test for the string side. The fixes are independent and can be co-landed in Task 3.
convergence_findings.confidence: HIGH (2 reviewers; both grounded in current ELSPETH conventions)
convergence_findings.resolution: In Task 3 (_derive_audit_characteristics): (a) replace _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG.get(determinism) with subscript access _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG[determinism] — KeyError is the correct Tier-1 crash for malformed enum. Add test_determinism_flag_map_is_exhaustive asserting set(_DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG.keys()) == set(Determinism). (b) Define VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS = frozenset({...known values...}) — either in service.py or a new contracts/audit_flags.py. Add a test in test_service_audit_derivation.py iterating all registered plugins and asserting every member of plugin_cls.audit_characteristics is in VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS. Mirrors the test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist pattern from Phase 2. Both fixes go in the same Task 3 commit; they share a regression-guard theme.

convergence_findings.title: Stale 'inferred-from-quarantine' language at 3-4 sites — purely textual, contradicts rev-2 decision
convergence_findings.evidence: 16a line 24 (Architecture summary): 'inferred from determinism and the source quarantine setting' — stale. Line 55 (scope boundary block): 'inferred-from-quarantine' — stale. Lines 1319-1321 (Task 5 commit message template): 'declared coerce + inferred io_read + inferred quarantine' — factual error; quarantine is author-declared. Lines 704-706 (test_transform_has_no_quarantine_inference docstring): 'quarantine is a source-only inference' — mischaracterizes the rule; quarantine is not inferred for any plugin type, it is author-declared in audit_characteristics. The implementation code in Task 3 is correct (omits quarantine inference); only the surrounding prose is stale. Line 1434 explicitly documents the rev-2 removal of the quarantine-inference rule.
convergence_findings.convergence_signal: Three reviewers agree the implementation is correct but the prose is stale. This is the trivial-fix category but matters because a future implementer reading the goal summary first will expect a feature that was deliberately removed.
convergence_findings.resolution: Documentation edit pass over 16a: lines 24, 55, 704-706 docstring, 1319-1321 commit-message template. ~5 lines of prose. No code or test changes.

major_issues.title: trust.py deletion commitment + 1-of-58 underscoping (convergence C1)
major_issues.issue: Phase 7A lands the data_trust_tier ClassVar with None defaults. trust.py's verbatim docstring (lines 31-32) commits to same-commit deletion when this ClassVar lands. The plan does not delete trust.py, does not update classify_plugin() callers, and does not name a successor phase for the 9 remaining EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins. CLAUDE.md No-Legacy policy + memory feedback_repeated_out_of_scope_is_underscoping both apply.
major_issues.resolution: Operator decision required (see operator-decision list). Synthesizer recommendation: Option A (author all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins in 16a Task 5, delete trust.py same commit) — this is the per-No-Legacy correct path and the additional 9 plugins are mechanical: each needs data_trust_tier=3 (transforms/sinks talking to external systems handle Tier-3 boundary data) plus the existing prose pattern. Estimated +2-3 commits in Task 5. Option B (revise trust.py docstring trigger condition + file 16a-supplement filigree blocker) is acceptable if operator deems Option A disproportionate.

major_issues.title: No mechanical vocabulary enforcement for audit_characteristics + closed-enum .get() drops future Determinism values (convergence C2)
major_issues.issue: Both surfaces of the audit-characteristic composition path lack mechanical guards. (a) _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG.get(determinism) silently returns None for unmapped values — exactly the silent-pass-through CLAUDE.md prohibits. (b) audit_characteristics is frozenset[str] with no membership constraint; an author typo silently disappears from the rendered card. Phase 7A establishes the vocabulary anchor that 57 subsequent plugin authors will copy.
major_issues.resolution: See C2 resolution. Both fixes co-land in Task 3 commit. ~10 lines for the enum exhaustivity guard, ~20 lines for the VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant + iteration test.

should_fix_before_merge.title: type: ignore[attr-defined] suppression on six new field accesses — Protocol update path not named
should_fix_before_merge.issue: _to_summary uses six consecutive # type: ignore[attr-defined] comments because the Phase-7A fields are not on SourceProtocol/TransformProtocol/SinkProtocol. The plan says 'remove them if mypy doesn't require them' but the Protocols are not being updated — the suppressions will be permanent. plugin_protocols.py is also not in the Task 6 mypy sweep. Latent risk: a future typo (usage_when_to_ue) is silently suppressed.
should_fix_before_merge.resolution: Preferred: add the six new ClassVar attributes to all three Protocol classes in contracts/plugin_protocols.py (L0 file; small addition; consistent with existing determinism/plugin_version/source_file_hash declarations on the Protocols) and add plugin_protocols.py to Task 6 mypy sweep. Alternative: introduce a narrow _HasReferenceContent Protocol locally and cast — documents the gap but leaves the structural inconsistency.

should_fix_before_merge.title: SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC Determinism cases not tested in derivation suite
should_fix_before_merge.issue: test_service_audit_derivation.py covers IO_READ, EXTERNAL_CALL, DETERMINISTIC, IO_WRITE — four of six Determinism values. SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC have no fixture. C2's exhaustivity test (if adopted) partially mitigates this but per-value behavioural pinning is still valuable.
should_fix_before_merge.resolution: Add _FakeSourceSeeded and _FakeTransformNonDeterministic fixtures and two test functions asserting 'seeded' and 'non_deterministic' respectively appear in the derived tuple.

should_fix_before_merge.title: data_trust_tier boundary-value tests incomplete
should_fix_before_merge.issue: Only data_trust_tier=7 (above upper bound) tested. Missing: 0 (below lower bound), -1 (negative), 1 (minimum valid), 3 (maximum valid as isolated boundary). Pydantic Field(ge=1, le=3) handles these correctly; the test suite should pin the design choice {1, 2, 3} explicitly.
should_fix_before_merge.resolution: Add four boundary cases to test_plugin_summary_rejects_invalid_trust_tier (or split into accepts/rejects pairs).

sources: convergence C3 — architecture W1+W2+W3, quality NIT-1, reality_hint M2
should_fix_before_merge.title: Stale 'inferred-from-quarantine' language at 4 textual sites
should_fix_before_merge.issue: Goal summary (line 24), scope-boundary block (line 55), Task 5 commit message template (line 1321), and test_transform_has_no_quarantine_inference docstring (lines 704-706) all carry rev-1 language about inferring quarantine from source settings. Rev-2 explicitly removed this rule; implementation code is correct.
should_fix_before_merge.resolution: See convergence finding C3 resolution. Documentation-only edit pass.

should_fix_before_merge.title: Wrong test class name TestSources (should be TestListSources)
should_fix_before_merge.issue: Dispatcher reports reality reviewer flagged that the plan references TestSources where the actual test class in tests/unit/web/catalog/test_routes.py is TestListSources. Synthesizer could not verify against the missing reality-review.json; flagged here for the implementer to confirm at first failing-test run.
should_fix_before_merge.resolution: grep 'class TestSources' tests/unit/web/catalog/test_routes.py — if absent, replace plan references with TestListSources. If the reality review file is recovered, re-confirm.

consider.title: No post-16c E2E smoke gate for the reshaped catalog drawer surface
consider.detail: 16-index does not specify an E2E Playwright verification gate after 16c. tests/e2e/llm-provider-schema.spec.ts is currently test.fixme'd (elspeth-dcf12c061b). Without an E2E smoke, Phase 7 ships a reshaped public-facing surface with only unit and component-level coverage. Demo risk: per project_rc5ux_demo_prep_scope the merge bar is 'demo succeeds.' Recommend the 16c plan (when written) include a post-implementation smoke that opens the drawer, confirms the CSV card shows non-null usage_when_to_use, and confirms the 'Use in pipeline' button is absent. Action: add to 16-index sequencing or defer to 16c plan author.

consider.title: data_trust_tier integer flows to LLM as bare number with no semantic framing
consider.detail: Starting from 16a merge, list_sources/transforms/sinks tool responses include data_trust_tier as 1/2/3/null. The plan correctly notes the tooltip explanation ships in 16b for UI; nothing ships for the LLM's interpretation channel. Risk: LLM reads 'data_trust_tier: 3' on CSVSource as 'low-trust source, avoid' rather than 'surfaces Tier-3 external data, which is normal for a source.' Mitigation: add two sentences to the pipeline-composer skill explaining the tier model's data-boundary semantics. Costs ~5 minutes, no plan change.

consider.title: LLM context bloat post-full-authoring — measure at Phase 8
consider.detail: Phase 7A's bounded impact: ~12% token increase in discovery tool responses (1/37 plugins annotated, 36 plugins serialize null fields adding ~6KB total). Becomes a medium-term concern when prose authoring scales. Recommended Phase 8 polish task: measure per-compose token counts once authoring reaches 10+ plugins; consider a PluginSummaryBrief for LLM-facing discovery if payload exceeds 30KB.

consider.title: Optional-by-design defaults vs No-Legacy policy — tradeoff comment recommended
consider.detail: The Phase 7A fields default to None / empty. This is the pragmatic choice for an incremental documentation surface, but tension-with-No-Legacy is unstated. Add a one-line comment in base.py at the new attribute block: 'Optional by deliberate design; if later made required, update all plugins in one commit per CLAUDE.md No-Legacy policy.' Costs nothing; makes the tradeoff discoverable.

consider.title: Strengthen Task 4 fake-plugin note from 'should work' to 'confirmed: _knob_schema returns {fields: []} for config_model=None'
consider.detail: Plan hedges with 'if validator rejects, swap to a minimal real plugin class.' Implementer might unnecessarily reach for real plugin classes. Note has been verified against service.py:127-128.

consider.title: Integration sweep adds one assertion on list_sources() return shape
consider.detail: Task 6 Step 2 integration sweep runs tests/integration/web/ but test_startup_lowering.py does not call list_sources/list_transforms/list_sinks. test_routes.py unit covers this against real plugins, so coverage is not zero. Optional one-line addition.

operator_decisions_required.title: trust.py deletion path — A, B, or C?
operator_decisions_required.context: Per M1 convergence (C1). Phase 7A as written ships the ClassVar but defers trust.py deletion to no named phase. The verbatim docstring commitment in trust.py:31-32 is triggered by this plan but not discharged.
options: A (PREFERRED per No-Legacy): Author data_trust_tier on all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins (5 transforms: llm, web_scrape, rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield; 5 sinks: azure_blob, chroma_sink, database, dataverse, plus any others surfaced by the grep) inside Phase 7A Task 5. Delete trust.py and replace classify_plugin() callers in the same commit. Discharges the No-Legacy obligation in this PR. Cost: +2-3 commits, +~50 lines per plugin (data_trust_tier=3 + brief prose).

B (ACCEPTABLE): Sibling-phase 16a-supplement. Rewrite trust.py:31-32 docstring to read 'when all EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins have data_trust_tier authored' (making the trigger condition truthful). File a filigree blocker tracking 16a-supplement that must close before trust.py deletion. Acknowledge in the plan's review history that this is a deliberate deferred-completion with operator override. Adds a row to 00-implementation-roadmap.md as 16a-supplement after 7C.

C (NOT RECOMMENDED): Delete the deletion-commitment language from trust.py entirely. Keep the allowlist as the permanent source of truth for the audit_readiness panel. Contradicts the design intent and CLAUDE.md No-Legacy.
operator_decisions_required.synthesizer_recommendation: A. The bulk-authoring work for 9 plugins is mechanical; the inflation of Phase 7A from 'schema + one canonical' to 'schema + canonical CSV + 9 trust-tier minimal annotations + trust.py deletion' is proportionate to discharging a load-bearing commitment. It also turns C1 from a future debt into a closed phase. Memory feedback_default_is_fix_not_ticket: 'default answer is never log a ticket.' Memory feedback_repeated_out_of_scope_is_underscoping: 'third-tasking no X is a flag, not authority; surface to operator.' Both point to A. However, if operator deems A scope-creep against the carefully-cut 7A/7B/7C boundary, B is acceptable provided the trust.py docstring is updated truthfully in the same Phase 7A commit and the filigree blocker is created before implementation starts.

operator_decisions_required.title: Phase 7 catalog finish-line — incremental per-PR, dedicated prose sprint, or accept fallback-mostly?
operator_decisions_required.context: Per systems W4. Even after Phase 7A/7B/7C ship, 57 of 58 plugins will render 'see the technical description' fallback in the catalog drawer. The roadmap names no successor phase for bulk prose authoring. Three paths exist; the plan does not say which is intended.
options: a (incremental per-PR): Every PR that touches a plugin must add or update its catalog prose. No dedicated sprint. Risk: prose ages with the plugin (correct staleness); 57 plugins authored over months.
b (dedicated prose sprint before demo): Schedule Phase 7D after 7C; author all registered plugins in one bulk commit-set. Risk: large sprint; may slip demo.

c (accept fallback-mostly): Phase 7's value is the filter/search surface (16c), not the prose. 08-catalog-reshape.md's framing ('the catalog is orientation material') needs revision to acknowledge that only ~5 high-value plugins (csv, llm, web_scrape, sql, blob) will be fully annotated for demo.
operator_decisions_required.synthesizer_recommendation: Surface this explicitly. If demo prep is the binding constraint (memory project_rc5ux_demo_prep_scope), recommend c — annotate the 5 demo-critical plugins in a small follow-up sprint between 7A and 7B, accept fallback for the rest. Update 08-catalog-reshape.md framing to match. This converges with OD1 Option A on csv_source + the 9 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins as the priority annotation set.

operator_decisions_required.title: Sibling-phase scope for 16a-supplement if OD1 = B
operator_decisions_required.context: If operator selects OD1 Option B, 16a-supplement needs a precise scope statement: which plugins, what prose density, who tracks completion, what gates trust.py deletion.
options: Scope: all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins (the trust.py allowlist union); minimal data_trust_tier + 1-paragraph usage_when_to_use; trust.py deletion blocked on completion; tracked as filigree epic with one issue per plugin.
Scope: only the 5 demo-critical plugins; remaining 5 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins keep allowlist entries permanently; trust.py becomes a permanent reduced allowlist for non-demo plugins.
Scope: defer entirely to post-demo; trust.py remains as-is.
operator_decisions_required.synthesizer_recommendation: Option-1 (all 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins). Cleanest discharge of the commitment; least drift risk. Only relevant if OD1 = B.

trust_py_addressability_inside_16a.question: Can the trust.py / 1-of-58 underscoping issue be addressed inside 16a, or should it be a sibling-phase commitment?
trust_py_addressability_inside_16a.rationale: The mechanical work is small: 10 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins (5 transforms + 4 sinks per the trust.py allowlist + 1 already-planned csv_source) need data_trust_tier=3 + 1-paragraph prose each. The plan's existing Task 5 already authors csv_source.py end-to-end; extending Task 5 to author the 9 additional plugins follows the same pattern. The trust.py deletion + classify_plugin() call-site update is a single commit. Estimated additional scope: ~2-3 commits, ~10-15 minutes of synthesizer-estimated implementer time per plugin for the trust-tier + brief usage prose. Total Task 5 expansion: csv_source canonical (full prose, demo-grade) + 9 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins (trust-tier + brief usage paragraph, sufficient for catalog drawer fallback to be readable). Phase 7A would ship with ~10/58 plugins annotated rather than 1/58 — still 'mostly fallback' for the broader catalog, but the No-Legacy commitment is discharged. The sibling-phase Option B (16a-supplement) is acceptable but introduces a second file, a filigree blocker, and a docstring revision in trust.py; Option A is cleaner. Synthesizer firmly recommends Option A. If operator selects Option B, the trust.py docstring revision MUST land in the same Phase 7A commit — leaving the verbatim commitment language unrevised while deferring the deletion is the No-Legacy violation.

what_is_good.plan_16_index: Three reviewers GO. Index is an honest map of three clean subplan boundaries with no hidden scope. Trust-tier semantics section (reading (a) vs (b)) pre-empts the most likely confusion point. Mergeability and parallel-shipping options correctly identified.
what_is_good.plan_16a_layer_compliance: L3 catalog imports L0 contracts.enums — downward-only. base.py modifications stay in L3. No upward imports. Task 6 Step 5 runs enforce_tier_model.py check.
what_is_good.plan_16a_tdd_shape: All five feature tasks follow failing-test-first with explicit pytest invocations. Task 5 Step 8 adds an end-to-end route test that pins the composed wire shape — closes the loop before the phase is declared done.
what_is_good.plan_16a_rev2_fix: The quarantine-cannot-be-inferred regression guard (test_quarantine_inference_is_not_attempted_from_instance_attribute) directly guards the rev-2 bug. Excellent defensive test.
what_is_good.plan_16a_tier_1_discipline: data_trust_tier: int | None = Field(default=None, ge=1, le=3) under _StrictResponse strict=True crashes correctly on malformed values. Matches CLAUDE.md Tier-1 offensive programming.
what_is_good.plan_16a_offensive_programming: Direct attribute access in _to_summary; no .get(), no getattr(), no try/except except the closed-enum .get() flagged in C2. The type:ignore suppressions are static-analysis workarounds, not defensive fallbacks.
what_is_good.plan_16a_blast_radius: 4 modified source files (2x weight on base.py mitigated by additive-only), 5 new test files. Weighted 7.0. Low-Medium risk.
what_is_good.plan_16a_no_one_way_doors: All schema additions are reversible. Frontend (16b) reads fields if present and falls back if absent. Removing the fields in a future commit is a clean deletion.

architecture.key_signal: trust.py No-Legacy violation (B1) is the load-bearing finding; W5 closed-enum .get() is the second-strongest. W1-W3 are documentation-only fixes to stale rev-1 prose.

quality.key_signal: TDD-shaped with good RED-GREEN discipline. Three test-coverage gaps (WARN-1 SEEDED/NON_DETERMINISTIC, WARN-2 boundary values, WARN-3 mypy on plugin_protocols). REC-1 post-16c E2E smoke gate is the strongest forward-looking recommendation.

systems.key_signal: M1 vocabulary enforcement is the load-bearing finding (mirrors Phase 2 W1 EXTERNAL_CALL marker drift, same archetype, same timing). W4 catalog-finish-line is the operator-communication gap. W1 LLM token bloat is bounded today but flagged for Phase 8.

reality.note: Dispatcher reports reality reviewer flagged M1 (wrong test class name TestSources vs TestListSources) and M2 (stale inferred-quarantine language). Other reality findings unknown. Re-synthesize if file is recovered. Reality typically catches fixture-shape errors and phantom symbols that the other three reviewers miss; absence of this review file is the largest residual uncertainty in this synthesis.

next_step: Operator decisions OD1 (trust.py path A/B/C) and OD2 (catalog finish-line a/b/c) must be answered before implementation starts. After decision: (1) If OD1=A, expand Task 5 in 16a to author 9 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY plugins; add trust.py deletion + classify_plugin() call-site update as new Task 5 final step. (2) Apply C2 fixes in Task 3 (subscript access + exhaustivity test for the Determinism map; VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS constant + iteration test). (3) Apply C3 stale-prose edits across the 4 sites. (4) Apply S1-S5 should-fix items. (5) If reality-review.json is recovered, re-synthesize before implementation. (6) Plan 16 (index) ships as-is; no changes required.
